headline: Equipment Rental Antitrust: Algorithmic Pricing Cases Sent to Illinois MDL
summary: A growing number of class action lawsuits alleging nationwide price-fixing in the construction equipment rental industry have been consolidated in the Northern District of Illinois (MDL No. 3152). These cases target major rental companies and Rouse Services LLC, their data analytics provider, for allegedly using shared, real-time algorithmic pricing to coordinate rental rates and inflate prices. The litigation draws parallels to similar cases against RealPage and Agri Stats, accusing benchmarking providers of facilitating collusion in other industries.
label: neutral

headline: Jennifer Garner’s Once Upon a Farm Files for IPO
summary: Once Upon a Farm, an organic baby food company co-founded by Jennifer Garner, has filed to go public on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker OFRM. The company is currently unprofitable, reporting a net loss of $28.5 million in the first half of 2025, but generated $110.6 million in revenue during the same period. Its IPO performance will likely be compared to Jessica Alba's Honest Company, which saw its stock price tumble after an initial $1.44 billion valuation in 2021.
label: neutral

headline: CI&T Releases “Retail Tech Reality Check” Report Highlightings AI’s Growing Role in Consumer Shopping
summary: CI&T has released its "Retail Tech Reality Check" report, which investigates the impact of artificial intelligence on consumer shopping behaviors. The report reveals a shift back to search engines for purchase journeys and a growing openness to agentic AI for routine buys, while also noting consumer caution regarding biased recommendations. Key findings indicate 58% of consumers believe retailers should use AI to enhance shopping experiences, and nearly three-quarters use AI tools in their path to purchase.
label: positive

headline: Home Depot expands pro offerings with digital project planner
summary: Home Depot has launched a new Project Planning tool, a digital platform designed to streamline project management for professional renovators, remodelers, and tradespeople. This tool allows pros to manage all project phases on one platform, access the full Home Depot product assortment, and collaborate with their support team. It includes features like comprehensive materials lists, consolidated transaction views, early pricing visibility, and multi-user collaboration.
label: positive

headline: The Lowe's Companies' (LOW) Resilient Business Model and its Importance for Retail Dividend Stocks
summary: Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) possesses a resilient business model due to its focus on renovations in an aging US housing market and a growing segment of professional contractors. The company is also a Dividend King, having increased its payouts for 60 consecutive years, making it an attractive retail dividend stock despite some housing market concerns.
label: negative

headline: A rebound for Home Depot and Lowe’s is ‘still a ways off’ even as rates ease, analysts say
summary: Oppenheimer analysts forecast that a quick recovery for Home Depot and Lowe's is unlikely despite easing interest rates. They suggest that the potential for lower rates will be gradual and lack the significant impact needed to stimulate the stagnant housing market. The analysts also noted that these retailers are still experiencing the aftermath of increased renovations during pandemic lockdowns.
label: neutral
